growing_areas: Areas Up To 500M Above Sea Level
soil_types: Well-Drained Soil Rich In Organic Matter

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Initially appears as light yellow spots on mature leaves. Spots enlarge with white powdery mycelium growth on the leaf surface. Severely affected leaves turn brown, shrivel, and drop.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected crop residue after harvest.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.impact: Angular yellow spots on the upper leaf surface, limited by leaf veins. Leaves become chlorotic and eventually necrotic, leading to host cell death.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected plant parts after harvest.

diseases.name: Foot Rot
diseases.impact: Plants show temporary wilting symptoms in the early stage. Leaves turn yellow, wilt, and droop. Eventually, the plant dies. Vascular bundles at the base turn yellow or brown. White fungal mycelium can be seen at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Improve drainage. Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Gummy Stem Blight
diseases.impact: Lesions typically start in water-soaked areas on leaves and fruits. Early stages may show chlorotic holes, which then turn light brown and become irregular. Dark, cracked, sunken spots appear on fruits. Sunken, cankerous lesions on stems may exude gum. Dark brown to black pycnidia can be seen on fruits, stems, or leaves.
diseases.solution: Deep plow crop residue immediately after harvest to reduce fungal survival. Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Sudden wilting of leaves, eventually the entire plant wilts. Leaves turn brown and dry. Vascular discoloration (browning) is observed. Leads to permanent wilting. Spread by infected soil, water, crop residue, nematodes, insects, pruning, and weed host plants. Diagnosis: White milky exudate from a cut stem submerged in clear water.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Practice crop rotation with resistant varieties (Okra, Maize). Implement mixed cropping with Cruciferae family crops. Avoid moving equipment from infected to uninfected fields.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves, shortening of internodes, and distortion. Leads to stunted plants and distorted fruits.
diseases.solution: Control Whitefly (Bemicia tabaci) vectors using recommended insecticides.

diseases.name: Papaya Ringspot Virus
diseases.impact: Mottled spots on leaves. Leaf distortion, fruit distortion, and reduced fruit size. In severe infections, oily spots appear on the fruit surface.
diseases.solution: Control Aphid vectors using recommended insecticides.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus
diseases.impact: Green mottling and distortion of leaves. Green spots on fruits, which turn bright yellow and decay. Fruit distortion.

diseases.name: Zucchini Yellow Mosaic Virus
diseases.impact: Stunted plants, yellowing, chlorosis (occasionally), mottled and distorted leaves (blistering). Fruit discoloration and distortion.

diseases.name: Phytoplasma Disease
diseases.impact: Small, thick, yellowish-green leaves. Shortened internodes and stunted plant growth. Phyllody (leaf-like floral organs) and virescence (greening of floral parts).
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants. Control Leafhopper vectors using recommended insecticides.
